settings.title: Little Oaks Introduces Forest School Fridays
settings.excerpt: Every Friday our older children now spend the morning learning outdoors, whatever the weather.
settings.body:
Children learn an enormous amount when they are outside with room to explore. To make more space for that, Little Oaks is adding a weekly outdoor session to our programme.

From this term, our preschool group spends Friday mornings at the nearby woodland for Forest School. They build dens, track minibeasts, and learn to take small, safe risks under the eye of two trained staff.

What the children get from it

Forest School is not just running about in the trees, though there is plenty of that. The morning is built to grow confidence and curiosity in ways the indoor room cannot quite match:

  • Building dens and working together to solve real problems.
  • Finding and naming minibeasts, and learning to look closely.
  • Judging small risks, like balancing on a log, with support nearby.

The point of the small, safe risks is that learning to manage them is how children grow steady and confident. A child who has carefully crossed a log a dozen times trusts their own body a little more.

How to send them ready

Please send wellies and a warm coat on Fridays, as we go out in light rain too. Layers work better than one thick jumper, and a spare pair of socks in the bag is always a good idea. A little mud, we promise, is part of the point.
